Container.Name: Prismarr
Container.Overview: One dashboard for your self-hosted media stack. Prismarr brings qBittorrent, Radarr, Sonarr, Prowlarr, Seerr and TMDb together in a single modern interface: unified Ctrl+K search, a merged movie/episode calendar, a dashboard that surfaces what matters today, and one settings page where every API key lives (stored in SQLite, never in environment variables). Ships as a single container with SQLite inside, no external database and no Redis. First boot opens a 7-step setup wizard: create the admin, plug your services in, done. IMPORTANT: the data volume must map to /var/www/html/var/data (Prismarr does NOT use the Servarr /config convention). All credentials are configured from the UI and kept in the database.
Container.Description: One dashboard for your self-hosted media stack (Radarr, Sonarr, Prowlarr, qBittorrent, Seerr, TMDb). Single container, SQLite inside, configured entirely from the UI. Map the data volume to /var/www/html/var/data.

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Persistent data: SQLite DB, auto-generated secrets, sessions, cache, avatars. Container target MUST stay /var/www/html/var/data.

Config.Name: TZ
Config.Description: IANA timezone for the clock and date helpers, e.g. Europe/Paris. Falls back to UTC if unset.

Config.Name: PHP_MEMORY_LIMIT
Config.Description: PHP memory ceiling per request. Bump (e.g. 2048M, or -1 for unlimited) for very large Radarr/Sonarr libraries.

Config.Name: PHP_MAX_EXECUTION_TIME
Config.Description: PHP wall-time ceiling per request, in seconds. Bump alongside PHP_MEMORY_LIMIT if the films/series page times out.

Config.Name: TRUSTED_PROXIES
Config.Description: Set to your reverse-proxy network (Traefik, nginx, Caddy, Cloudflare Tunnel) so Symfony reads the right X-Forwarded-* headers. Leave empty otherwise.
